titleEn: Pig ProgressSouth Korea’s CAVAC receives export licence for ASF vaccineJun 22Article
titleZh: 韩国CAVAC获非洲猪瘟疫苗出口许可，瞄准越南和菲律宾市场
summaryZh: 韩国动物保健公司CAVAC开发的非洲猪瘟（ASF）疫苗Suishot ASF-X已获韩国出口许可，预计未来几个月将在越南和菲律宾获得上市批准。该疫苗为细胞适应活疫苗，针对基因2型病毒，可降低临床症状和死亡率，并可作为诱饵疫苗用于野猪群体控制。CAVAC选择越南和菲律宾作为首批市场，因其对ASF控制需求迫切，而韩国因采取零容忍政策暂不适用疫苗接种策略。
featuredReason: 非洲猪瘟疫苗的突破对全球养猪业至关重要，CAVAC采用自然减毒株的差异化路径值得关注，其诱饵疫苗设计也为野猪防控提供了新思路。
contentFull: South Korea’s CAVAC receives export licence for ASF vaccineA vaccine of South Korean origin against African Swine Fever (ASF) is soon to hit the market in the Philippines and Vietnam. In April 2026, animal health company CAVAC received export permission for its novel vaccine called Suishot ASF-X in South Korea.Vincent ter BeekEditor of Pig Progress / Topic: Pigs around the worldJun 22ArticleMarketing permission in both countries is expected to be granted over the next few months, and the registration process is now under way, explained the company’s CEO Dr Jason Lee and Dr Sungsik Yoo, the head of the ASF project at CAVAC. The company shared this message at the 28th edition of the International Pig Veterinary Society (IPVS) congress, held June 15-18 in Ho Chi Minh City, Vietnam.Dr Jason Lee.Dr Sungsik Yoo.Protection against genotype 2The vaccine is developed to provide protection against genotype 2 of the virus, and in its official communication brochure the vaccine is said to ‘reduce clinical signs including mortality and provide robust protection’.The vaccine is promoted as a two-shot 2 ml intramuscular injection and can be administered to any type of animal, i.e. piglets, gilts or sows, even though the suggested administration schedule for each animal type is slightly different.The vaccine is a ‘cell-adapted live vaccine’, called ASFv-MEC-01. In doing so, the company has taken a different road than most other vaccine development initiatives, i.e. it is not building on basic research carried out at the Agricultural Research Service (ARS) of the US Department of Agriculture (USDA). Instead, the company chose to work with a naturally occurring attenuated version of the virus that was observed to circulate in wild boar in South Korea.The vaccine is being introduced to the market not only as a vaccine for domestic animals, but also as a ‘bait vaccine’, so it could be used for virus control in the wild boar population. The company has experience with making bait vaccines as it has produced similar products for the control of both Classical Swine Fever (CSF) and rabies.Vietnam and the PhilippinesThe company has opted for markets like Vietnam and the Philippines as that is where there is an urgent need to get the virus under control. Its home market South Korea – which has also been affected by ASF since 2019 – follows a national control policy that is more in line with the EU approach of zero tolerance. A vaccination strategy in South Korea is therefore not in line with the market needs.At a satellite symposium at IPVS, CAVAC’s employees emphasised the product’s safety, efficacy and genetic stability. Field trials have been carried out at farms in Vietnam near both Ho Chi Minh City (piglets, gestating sows and newborn piglets) as well as Hanoi (piglets and gilts). When pregnant sows were vaccinated, outcomes reported a 100% seroconversion amongst neonatal piglets after post-colostrum ingestion.In trials with six-week-old pigs carried out in Vietnam, no significant changes in body temperature or abnormal clinical signs were observed following both prime and booster vaccinations. In addition, all vaccinated animals seroconverted 1 week after the booster vaccination. Last but not least, all vaccinated animals were observed to survive after a challenge with ASF genotype II, whereas in the control group all animals had died.The company stated that the vaccine has been developed in alignment with guidelines of the World Organisation of Animal Health (WOAH).Other ASF vaccine projectsOnce on the international market, it means that after various attempts from Vietnam, now also a player from South Korea will enter with an ASF vaccine. At the IPVS Congress, also MSD Animal Health (Merck in the USA) shared updates about their development towards an ASF vaccine – and so did Komipharm, another South Korean animal health company. Since 2023, the Vietnamese company AVAC has been promoting its vaccine for use in piglets in various markets in South East Asia.
translationZh:
韩国CAVAC公司开发的非洲猪瘟（ASF）疫苗即将在菲律宾和越南上市。2026年4月，动物保健公司CAVAC在韩国获得了其新型疫苗Suishot ASF-X的出口许可。

该公司CEO Jason Lee博士和ASF项目负责人Sungsik Yoo博士表示，预计未来几个月内将在两国获得上市许可，注册程序正在进行中。该消息是在6月15日至18日于越南胡志明市举行的第28届国际猪兽医学会（IPVS）大会上分享的。

**针对基因2型的保护**
该疫苗旨在提供对病毒基因2型的保护，其官方宣传手册称该疫苗能“减少包括死亡在内的临床症状，并提供强有力的保护”。

该疫苗被推广为两针2毫升肌肉注射，可适用于任何类型的动物，即仔猪、后备母猪或经产母猪，尽管每种动物类型的建议接种方案略有不同。

该疫苗是一种“细胞适应活疫苗”，名为ASFv-MEC-01。在这一点上，该公司选择了与大多数其他疫苗开发项目不同的道路，即它并非基于美国农业部农业研究服务局（ARS）的基础研究。相反，该公司选择使用一种在韩国野猪中观察到的自然减毒病毒株。

该疫苗不仅作为家畜疫苗推向市场，还作为“诱饵疫苗”用于野猪群体的病毒控制。该公司在制作诱饵疫苗方面有经验，曾生产过用于控制经典猪瘟（CSF）和狂犬病的类似产品。

**越南和菲律宾**
该公司选择越南和菲律宾作为市场，因为这些地区迫切需要控制病毒。其本土市场韩国——自2019年以来也受到ASF影响——遵循的是与欧盟零容忍方法一致的国家控制政策。因此，在韩国实施疫苗接种策略不符合市场需求。

在IPVS的一次卫星研讨会上，CAVAC的员工强调了产品的安全性、有效性和遗传稳定性。田间试验已在越南胡志明市附近（仔猪、妊娠母猪和新生仔猪）以及河内（仔猪和后备母猪）的农场进行。当妊娠母猪接种疫苗后，结果显示新生仔猪在摄入初乳后血清转化率达到100%。

在越南进行的六周龄仔猪试验中，
author: Vincent ter Beek
